content.problemStatement:
Let us find out more details about highly rated dishes.

### Task
Write a query to do the following. Try and use the concept of sub-queries.
- You need to output details of the dish - 'f_name', 'f_cost' and 'f_type' ONLY if the following condition is satisfied
- Average rating of the dish is greater than or equal to 4
```sql
Expected output
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ     f_name      в”Ӯ f_cost в”Ӯ   f_type    в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ Pizza           в”Ӯ 10     в”Ӯ Continental в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Fried Rice      в”Ӯ 12     в”Ӯ Chinese     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Pad Thai        в”Ӯ 14     в”Ӯ Thai        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Sushi           в”Ӯ 20     в”Ӯ Japanese    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Beef Stroganoff в”Ӯ 18     в”Ӯ Russian     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Paella          в”Ӯ 25     в”Ӯ Spanish     в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

### Table Formats
Table 'food' has the following columns: 
- f_id (int)
- f_name (text)
- f_cost (int)
-  f_type (int).

Table 'ratings' has the following columns: 
- f_id (int)
- f_rating (text).
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ вҖ” the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
